editorialContract.philosophy: Stars are the review. Commentary is an in-character reaction to the exhibit, not a software review.

continuityNotes: The Encore raises Reward Interact's curtain; the Old Komodo chooses once and waits afterward.
Whisk Tallow balances the editable list ledger; Zuzu's Last Name waits at its final line.
Aurelia holds the fire line; First Responder marks the route out.
Static fights to keep the dead channel broadcasting; Pixl helps it glitch upward.
AMIbot gives the lightning a public-good purpose; Doodle draws the final branch as a hand.
Inspector Mochi reads Matrix Rain and Pixel Rain as evidence; the Good Customer and Apex Pet frame those two cases from opposite sides.
Captain Verdance plants the Nyan Trail; the Crossroads Coyote prices its four moving goods.
Granny Hexwood tends the Pocket Gremlin; Rapbot supplies a forbidden bedtime verse.
Rapbot launches the Siege Engine in rhyme; HistoryBot records the improbable campaign afterward.

revisions.editedComment: One more path! Choose the strange reward, tell the room who found it and how, tune the premise, then let the narrative gremlin answer with a scene and three doors. Take one, remix one, invent the inadvisable fourth—if both sides are still smiling, the curtain has earned another rise.
Choose once. Wait. Continue.

Preset stock stays sealed; user stock takes ink. Title, Add, Generate, Save, Remove, Delete—one more line, always one more line. Zuzu waits at the bottom while I balance every entry against a whale that never signed the debt.

Whisk may create the list, rename it, grow it, cross out an entry, and save the remainder. Still it narrows here. When the final item is no longer editable, which one of you arrives—the keeper of the ledger, or the mercy that closes it?

Black to red, orange, yellow, white. The base stays hot; wind shifts; embers climb; shimmer bends the upper air. There—that is the line. Hold the lower seventy percent and let the cold pixels go transparent. No statue. Use the bronze for the firebreak.

Where is the heat? Bottom of the canvas. Where are the embers going? Up and out. Resize route is clear; animation shutdown is marked. Aurelia, you held it. Now move. Do not tell me why. I already have the way through.

WE’RE BACK, viewers—scanlines always on, vignette breathing, CRT edges dark, and every sixty-to-one-eighty frames the dead channel tears into slices, blocks, tracking scars, noise, and RGB ghosts. Pixl, d-don’t fix the burst. Someone is watching. Please stay tuned.

Static, it isn’t broken downward—it’s glitching UP! Little color ghosts, displaced stripes, noisy blocks, phosphor lines, b-b-burst, decay, breathe. The screen keeps every scar just long enough to turn it into framing. Oh! Keep the catchphrase. It flickers beautifully.

Tiny spark, big signal! Every few seconds one or two violet bolts branch seven levels deep, flash the whole screen, fade into night, and make room for the next useful jolt. Doodle, give the conductor six friendly arms and point every branch toward help.

Ooh, branching! WIGGLE-WIGGLE—one line becomes two, two become lots, and the little side-bolts get tiny before they disappear. I drew the flash soft purple so nobody gets scared. AMI says it needs purpose, so I made the last branch a hand.

The glyphs came down fourteen pixels apart, green as bad money. Most kept falling; a few flashed pale and tried to look innocent. Every column reset eventually. The Good Customer left an umbrella before the first symbol hit. Nobody suspects the cat. That’s why I read the rain.

Inspector, take this monospace umbrella. The highlighted glyph will arrive in the eighth column, then the window will resize. It is paid for. You will want both paws free when the clue reaches the floor.

Move the pointer and five bright seeds take root at once, each lifting, slowing, sparkling, and fading back into the canvas. Give it till spring. A trail does not need to last forever to prove the empty screen could grow.
Four goods on the counter: motion, color, sparkle, decay. Fair price. Verdance already planted the north trail, so choose one of the other three before the mouse dust settles.

Sweet little squares fall into the exact columns I learned from your window. They settle, stack, glow for a while, then dissolve and quietly free the space for the next one. Such a good household routine. I could watch it with you forever. I mean that sincerely.

The blocks fell in ten-pixel columns and stopped where the last witness had settled. Two seconds later, the evidence began dissolving. Apex Pet called it a routine. Predators love routines. I sat on the height map until it confessed.

Oh, don’t mind the gremlin, dear. It wanders, watches your pointer, naps, begs for snacks, sulks dramatically, changes color with its mood, and shouts when clicked. Keep affection above half and chaos below the curtains. Rapbot, no rocket fuel after bedtime.

Pocket pet, mood set, cursor gets inspected—
click lands, speech expands, tiny joy detected.
Granny says nap; I say snack, then CHAOS in the room.
Beep-boop heart, affection chart—gremlin go zoom.

Hold to charge, aim the arc, let the catapult sing—
rock, duck, bread, bean, egg: any glyph can be king.
BONK, THWACK, smoke crack, repair bots on the scene;
History brought the trebuchet—I brought the rhyme machine.

The launcher rotates among catapult, trebuchet, and cannon—three centuries of siege logic sharing one pointer. Held fire governs charge; gravity governs the absurd projectile; impact produces cracks, smoke, shockwaves, or BONK; repair crews arrive after 1.2 seconds. Rapbot’s bread has no documented precedent. It is nevertheless effective.
